description_long: This is a cylindrical stone tower with a conical, shingled roof and timber-framed sections dividing its height into three main segments. The exterior features a mix of rough stone and timber beams, giving it a medieval, half-timbered appearance. There are small, arched windows on each level, with the lower and middle levels each having a single window visible from the front. The base is slightly wider, forming a circular stone foundation. The structure is reinforced with horizontal wooden bands at each level, and the roof is steeply pitched with a textured, weathered look. The overall design suggests a watchtower or lookout post, likely found in a historical or fantasy urban setting.
description: A cylindrical, medieval-style stone and timber tower with a conical shingled roof and arched windows on each level.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main arched entrance at the base and two arched windows above, with timber framing and stonework visible.
description_view_1: Side view with no visible windows, highlighting the timber framing and stone texture along the cylindrical body.
description_view_2: Rear view with two arched windows, one at the base and one at the middle level, and prominent timber bands.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with no windows, showing the continuous timber and stone pattern.
